claim: Agentic workflows with Claude Code self-correct when errors occur—the agent reads the error, understands the problem, and rewrites its own code to fix it without manual intervention
actionable: Let the agent handle runtime errors instead of manually debugging; expect ~80% of issues to resolve automatically
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)

claim: The agentic stack has three layers: directive (what you want), orchestration (AI figures out steps/tools), and execution (AI writes and tests code)
actionable: Structure your automation requests in three parts: state the goal, let the AI plan the steps, and let it implement without you touching the code
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)

claim: Traditional no-code tools (n8n, Zapier, Make) create visual spaghetti—when one block changes due to API updates or field renames, the entire workflow breaks
actionable: Replace fragile no-code workflows with agentic systems that can adapt to API changes and schema updates automatically
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)

claim: Claude Code can access MCP documentation (like Context 7) and learn APIs on its own, eliminating manual documentation reading and trial-and-error
actionable: Provide API documentation to Claude Code via MCP instead of reading it yourself; it will write working integrations in minutes
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)

claim: Claude Code can process multiple workflows in parallel while maintaining separate context spaces, allowing you to build many automations without sequential bottlenecks
actionable: Open multiple Claude Code sessions to parallelize automation development; describe each workflow and let the agent work on all simultaneously
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)

claim: With agentic workflows, you shift from being the orchestrator (manually defining every step) to being the director (defining the goal and letting the agent figure out steps)
actionable: Stop writing detailed step-by-step instructions; instead, state your desired outcome and let the agent break it into subtasks
source_title: DON'T Build n8n workflows, build Agentic Workflows! (Claude Code)
